frameworks.name: attractor network dynamics
frameworks.one_line: decision-making mediated by neural networks with attractor dynamics

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: pulsatile auditory evidence is integrated toward a perceptual choice

frameworks.name: unsupervised deep-learning dynamics discovery
frameworks.one_line: data-driven method extracts latent decision-related dynamics from neural populations

frameworks.name: ramping vs stepping single-neuron models
frameworks.one_line: diverse single-neuron temporal profiles reconciled by one population-level model

core_question: What dynamical regimes underlie neuronal activity during perceptual decision-making, and when is the decision committed?
key_assumptions: perceptual decisions are mediated by neural networks with attractor dynamics
population-level trajectories reveal sensory-driven vs autonomous regimes
a neurally inferred time of commitment (nTc) can be estimated per trial
manipulations: pulsatile auditory evidence accumulation task in rats
simultaneous recording of hundreds of neurons in frontal cortex and striatum
trial-by-trial alignment to estimated nTc vs stimulus/response timing
key_findings: neural trajectories evolve in two sequential regimes: sensory-driven then autonomous
the autonomous flow direction is largely orthogonal to the sensory-driven mode
estimated nTc varies broadly across trials and is not locked to stimulus or response
evidence before nTc influenced choice while evidence after did not
interpretations: the regime transition marks the moment of decision commitment
a simple dynamical model can capture diverse ramping and stepping single-neuron profiles
